content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You may change any number of its elements (possibly zero), obtaining a new sequence of positive integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã. Each element of BBB must be an integer between 222 and 505050 (both inclusive).

Let's define an undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). For each pair of different vertices iii and jjj, there is an edge between iii and jjj if and only if BiB_iBi‚Äã and BjB_jBj‚Äã are coprime.

You should choose the sequence BBB in such a way that GGG is a connected graph. The number of elements which need to be changed to obtain BBB from AAA should be minimum possible. Find one such sequence BBB and the minimum required number of changes.

It can be proven that a solution always exists ‚Äî it is always possible to modify sequence AAA in such a way that GGG is connected.
constraints: `Explanation**Example 1**: There is an edge in GGG between vertices 111 and 222. This graph is connected, so there is no need to change any elements.

**Example 2**: There is no edge between vertices 111 and 222 since gcd(2,4)‚âÝ1\mathrm{gcd}(2, 4) \neq 1gcd(2,4)ÓÄÝ=1. This graph is not connected. We can change element A2=4A_2=4A2‚Äã=4 to 333 and make this graph connected.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines. The first line should contain a single integer ‚Äî the minimum required number of changes. The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã ‚Äî the modified sequence.

If there are multiple solutions, you may print any one.
